englishDefinitions: A term used to describe the national mood of doubt, caution, or loss of confidence in the United States after the Vietnam War.
A political or cultural feeling of reluctance to support military action or foreign intervention after the Vietnam experience.
persianTranslation: حالت بی‌اعتمادی و احتیاط پس از جنگ ویتنام
examples: Politicians often discussed post Vietnam syndrome in the late 1970s.
The country’s hesitation to use force abroad was sometimes linked to post Vietnam syndrome.
This book examines how post Vietnam syndrome shaped American politics.
